title: Workflow Syntax, Expressions, YAML, and Data Contracts · Delivery-System Trace: Ci Yaml Scalars Multiline Keys
summary: A workflow parses but triggers unexpectedly, treats false as text, expands a multiline output incorrectly, inherits a permissive default, and hides a misspelled key. Planned artifact: Add and verify the ci yaml scalars multiline keys increment in validated workflow data contract. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Control YAML scalars, booleans, quoting, multiline blocks, reserved-looking keys, indentation, and serialization boundaries.
Author name, on, permissions, concurrency, env, defaults, jobs, and job-level contracts with explicit supported syntax.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

content.code:
# Worked focus: ci-yaml-scalars-multiline-keys — Control YAML scalars, booleans, quoting, multiline blocks, reserved-looking keys, indentation, and serialization boundaries.
  # Evidence: ci-worked-gha-workflow-syntax-expressions-2

    artifact: workflow-contract.yaml
    decision: worked gha workflow syntax expressions 2
    environment: trusted release tag requiring an environment approval
    fault-injection: route five percent traffic to a revision with a data incompatibility
    retained-proof: accessible redacted timeline with owner expiry and regression control
    case-key: worked2
    change: |
      name: verified-ci
      on-events: [pull_request, push]
      permissions: contents-read
      concurrency: revision-scoped
      expression-input: parsed-with-fromJSON
      shell-input: validated-environment
      schema: action-inputs-reviewed
      changed-event-fixture: fork-pull-request
    verify: validate workflow schema expressions context availability action inputs and changed event fixtures
    failure: Reject valid-YAML-only review, implicit coercion, unavailable contexts, or direct untrusted expression interpolation.
    repair: Validate workflow semantics, parse explicit types, move untrusted values through data boundaries, and test changed payloads.
